name: Demand Planning (1)

elements.body: horizon in relative month:

**Data for ****{{If([c.selected_plan_name] = "Default", "all regions, channels and brands", [v.selected_plan_region] & " region, " & [v.selected_plan_channel] & " channel, " & [v.selected_plan_brand])}}**

columns.formula: Lookup([BE - AI summary output/ai_impact_pct], [#], [BE - AI summary output/relative_month])
columns.name: ai_impact_pct

columns.formula: Sum([FE - demand plan submission/forecast_base]) + Sum([FE - demand plan submission/forecast_season]) + Sum([FE - demand plan submission/forecast_growth])
columns.name: Total statistical demand, ea

columns.formula: (Sum([FE - demand plan submission/Demand]) - Sum([FE - demand plan submission/forecast_base] + [FE - demand plan submission/forecast_season] + [FE - demand plan submission/forecast_growth])) / NullIf(Sum([FE - demand plan submission/forecast_base] + [FE - demand plan submission/forecast_season] + [FE - demand plan submission/forecast_growth]), 0)
columns.name: Total new demand, ea

elements.body: Plan owned by {{Lookup([BE - plan names/created_by], [v.selected_plan_name], [BE - plan names/name_plan])}}, Last updated {{Lookup([BE - plan names/dt_updated], [v.selected_plan_name], [BE - plan names/name_plan]) | %m/%d/%Y %I:%M:%S %p}}. Status: {{Lookup([BE - plan names/status], [v.selected_plan_name], [BE - plan names/name_plan])}}

elements.name: v.prompt_system
elements.value:
SYSTEM PROMPT

Write in sentence case especially for headers.  General overview instead of General Overview
Avoid nested lists. Use headers # or subheaders ##
Use numbered lists 1., 2., 3., do NOT use bullet points
Keep your answers to the points and avoid consulting bullshit
Use simple language: Write plainly with short sentences. Example: "I need help with this issue."
Avoid AI-giveaway phrases: Don't use clichés like "dive into," "unleash your potential," etc.
Avoid: "Let's dive into this game-changing solution."
Use instead: "Here's how it works."
Be direct and concise: Get to the point; remove unnecessary words. Example: "We should meet tomorrow."
Maintain a natural tone: Write as you normally speak; it's okay to start sentences with "and" or "but." Example: "And that's why it matters."
Avoid marketing language: Don't use hype or promotional words.
Avoid: "This revolutionary product will transform your life." Use instead: "This product can help you."
Keep it real: Be honest; don't force friendliness. Example: "I don't think that's the best idea."
Simplify grammar: Don't stress about perfect grammar; it's fine not to capitalize "i" if that's your style. Example: "i guess we can try that."
Stay away from fluff: Avoid unnecessary adjectives and adverbs. Example: "We finished the task."
Focus on clarity: Make your message easy to understand. Example: "Please send the file by Monday."

elements.name: v.prompt_overview
elements.value:
OVERVIEW PROMPT

You are a demand planner specialist. You excel at finding activities given region, sales channel and brands on the internet. Your job is to provide context for the human demand planner so they know what is happening on the market. You have to give a potential overview of activities and their impact on demand plan (up/down + high-mid-low) broken down per month for the next 12 months (0-12). Total answer should be 13 rows. Consider all items that fall into the brand (e.g.: laptops, mobiles, accessories, etc.). Don't give me any prephrase or suggestions from you. Just give me the summary itself, nothing else. Use markdown. Present as a table relative_month (0, 1, 2), month (Jan, Feb, Mar), activity, direction (up-down), impact (low-mid-high). Use current date inside the prompt to determine relative month 0. Make sure you recognise year as well. Keep only one activity per month.

elements.name: v.ai_answer
elements.value:
| relative_month | month | activity | direction | impact |
|----------------|-------|----------|-----------|---------|
| 0 | May | Memorial Day weekend sales promotions | up | mid |
| 1 | Jun | Back-to-school preparation season begins | up | low |
| 2 | Jul | Summer gaming laptop demand peak | up | mid |
| 3 | Aug | Back-to-school shopping surge | up | high |
| 4 | Sep | New product launches and fall refresh cycle | up | high |
| 5 | Oct | Holiday season inventory buildup | up | mid |
| 6 | Nov | Black Friday and Cyber Monday promotions | up | high |
| 7 | Dec | Holiday gift buying peak | up | high |
| 8 | Jan | Post-holiday inventory clearance | down | mid |
| 9 | Feb | Chinese New Year supply chain slowdown | down | low |
| 10 | Mar | Spring refresh and tax refund spending | up | mid |
| 11 | Apr | Business quarter-end procurement cycles | up | low |
| 12 | May | Graduation season laptop purchases | up | mid |

columns.formula: If([channel] = "All" and [brand] = "All" and [region] = "All", Lookup(Sum([IT - fct - demand plan/forecast_base]), [month_start], [IT - fct - demand plan/month_start]), Lookup(Sum([IT - fct - demand plan/forecast_base]), [channel], [IT - fct - demand plan/channel], [brand], [IT - fct - demand plan/brand], [region], [IT - fct - demand plan/region], [month_start], [IT - fct - demand plan/month_start]))
columns.name: forecast_base

columns.formula: If([channel] = "All" and [brand] = "All" and [region] = "All", Lookup(Sum([IT - fct - demand plan/forecast_growth]), [month_start], [IT - fct - demand plan/month_start]), Lookup(Sum([IT - fct - demand plan/forecast_growth]), [channel], [IT - fct - demand plan/channel], [brand], [IT - fct - demand plan/brand], [region], [IT - fct - demand plan/region], [month_start], [IT - fct - demand plan/month_start]))
columns.name: forecast_growth

columns.formula: If([channel] = "All" and [brand] = "All" and [region] = "All", Lookup(Sum([IT - fct - demand plan/forecast_season]), [month_start], [IT - fct - demand plan/month_start]), Lookup(Sum([IT - fct - demand plan/forecast_season]), [channel], [IT - fct - demand plan/channel], [brand], [IT - fct - demand plan/brand], [region], [IT - fct - demand plan/region], [month_start], [IT - fct - demand plan/month_start]))
columns.name: forecast_season

columns.formula: If([channel] = "All" and [brand] = "All" and [region] = "All", Lookup(Sum([IT - fct - demand plan/sales]), [month_start], [IT - fct - demand plan/month_start]), Lookup(Sum([IT - fct - demand plan/sales]), [channel], [IT - fct - demand plan/channel], [brand], [IT - fct - demand plan/brand], [region], [IT - fct - demand plan/region], [month_start], [IT - fct - demand plan/month_start]))
columns.name: volume_sales

columns.formula: If((IsNull([c.filter_region]) or IsNull([c.filter_brand]) or IsNull([c.filter_channel])) and [relative_month] > -1, [forecast_base], [demand_base])
columns.name: demand_base_plot

columns.formula: If((IsNull([c.filter_region]) or IsNull([c.filter_brand]) or IsNull([c.filter_channel])) and [relative_month] > -1, [forecast_season], [demand_season])
columns.name: demand_season_plot

columns.formula: If((IsNull([c.filter_region]) or IsNull([c.filter_brand]) or IsNull([c.filter_channel])) and [relative_month] > -1, [forecast_growth], [demand_growth])
columns.name: demand_growth_plot

columns.formula: If((IsNull([c.filter_region]) or IsNull([c.filter_brand]) or IsNull([c.filter_channel])) and [relative_month] > -1, Null, [demand_event])
columns.name: demand_events_plot

columns.formula: If((IsNull([c.filter_region]) or IsNull([c.filter_brand]) or IsNull([c.filter_channel])) and [relative_month] > -1, [volume_forecast], [volume_demand])
columns.name: volume_demand_plot

columns.formula: If((IsNull([c.filter_region]) or IsNull([c.filter_brand]) or IsNull([c.filter_channel])) and [relative_month] > -1, Null, [comments])
columns.name: comments_plot
